response:
## Agent context check: missing

`crimes scan` flagged this repo with **crime_00009 — Missing Agent Context** (medium, confidence 0.9, agent_risk 0.61):

- No `AGENTS.md` at repo root
- No `CLAUDE.md` at repo root
- No `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- `package.json` declares bins (`tsup`, `tsup-node`) with no agent-discoverable docs

## Consequence for agent work

An agent landing here has to infer everything from the file tree: that this is `tsup` (a bundler built on esbuild + rollup-for-dts), that `src/index.ts` is the orchestrator, that `src/utils.ts` is load-bearing (fan-in 18), and that several files are intentionally large (`src/index.ts:build` is 293 lines, `src/esbuild/index.ts:runEsbuild` is 237). Without that context, agents will:

- Pick the wrong test runner / build command
- Refactor God Functions that are large for non-obvious reasons
- Miss that `src/plugin.ts` (fan-in 10) and `src/utils.ts` (fan-in 18) ripple widely
- Re-add `Date.now()` calls that the team has already tolerated
- Not know that `.test.ts` files use the local `test/utils.ts` harness (fan-in 9)

The eight High God Function findings and the High Fan-In files become guesswork without orientation.

## Minimal AGENTS.md skeleton

```markdown
# AGENTS.md

## What this is
tsup — bundler powered by esbuild (JS/TS) and rollup (.d.ts).
Public entrypoint: `src/index.ts` (build orchestrator).
CLI entrypoint: `src/cli-main.ts`.

## Commands
- Install: `pnpm install`
- Build: `pnpm build`            # tsup builds itself
- Test:  `pnpm test`             # vitest
- Lint:  `pnpm lint`
- Typecheck: `pnpm typecheck`
Run the test you touched, not the full suite, when iterating.

## Layout
- `src/index.ts`         — main `build()` orchestrator; large by design
- `src/cli-main.ts`      — argv → options → build
- `src/esbuild/`         — esbuild plugins (postcss, svelte, swc, …)
- `src/rollup.ts`        — rollup config builder for .d.ts
- `src/api-extractor.ts` — Microsoft API Extractor integration
- `src/plugin.ts`        — internal plugin contract (fan-in 10)
- `src/utils.ts`         — shared helpers (fan-in 18, load-bearing)
- `test/utils.ts`        — test harness used by every spec

## Load-bearing files (read before editing)
- `src/utils.ts`         — 18 importers
- `src/plugin.ts`        — 10 importers, defines plugin lifecycle
- `src/log.ts`           — 9 importers
- `test/utils.ts`        — 9 importers (test contract)

## Conventions
- Plugins return esbuild/rollup-shaped objects; don't widen those return types.
- `removeFiles`, `setSilent`, and `createLogger` have side effects despite
  innocuous names — keep them in orchestrator code, not pure helpers.
- Long functions in `src/index.ts`, `src/esbuild/index.ts`, and
  `src/rollup.ts` are tolerated; prefer additive edits over splitting.

## Don't
- Don't introduce new sync I/O (`*Sync`) in domain code; async exists.
- Don't add `Date.now()` / `new Date()` outside logging — bundle timings
  are the only sanctioned use.
- Don't restructure `src/utils.ts` or `src/plugin.ts` without checking
  importers first.

## Release
Changesets-driven. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for the release flow.
```

That clears `crime_00009`: any one of `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or a `SKILL.md` satisfies the detector. AGENTS.md is the most portable across coding agents.
